subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following statement(s) is (are)
incorrect reason for eutrophication?
(A) excess usage of fertilisers
(B) excess usage of detergents
(C) dense plant population in water bodies
(D) lack of nutrients in water bodies that prevent plant growth

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Which of the following statement(s) is (are)
incorrect reason for eutrophication?
(A) excess usage of fertilisers
(B) excess usage of detergents
(C) dense plant population in water bodies
(D) lack of nutrients in water bodies that prevent plant growth

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution: The process in which nutrient enriched water bodies support a dense plant population which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen and results in subsequent loss of biodiversity is known as eutrophication.

subject: Chemistry
q: Green chemistry in day-to-day life is in the use of:
question: Green chemistry in day-to-day life is in the use of:
options: Large amount of water alone for washing clothes
Liquified <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> for dry cleaning of clothes
solution:
Chlorine gas was used earlier for bleaching paper. These days, hydrogen peroxide H2O2 is used with suitable catalyst.

Tetrachloroethene Cl2C=CCl2 was earlier used as solvent for dry cleaning. The compound contaminates the ground water and is also a suspected carcinogen. Replacement of halogenated solvent by liquid CO2 will result in less harm to groundwater. Hence, given statement (4) is correct.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The satements that are TRUE:
(A) Methane leads to both global warming and photochemical smog
(B) Methane is generated from paddy fields
(C) Methane is a stronger global warming gas than CO2
(D) Methane is a part of reducing smog

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
The satements that are TRUE:
(A) Methane leads to both global warming and photochemical smog
(B) Methane is generated from paddy fields
(C) Methane is a stronger global warming gas than CO2
(D) Methane is a part of reducing smog

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
solution: Methane leads to both global warming & photochemical smog. Methane is generated in large amounts from paddy fields. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> can be absorbed by photosynthesis, or by formation of acid rain etc., while no such activities are there for methane. Hence methane is stronger global warming gas than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, Methane is not a component of reducing smog.

subject: Chemistry
q: Correct statement about smog is
question: Correct statement about smog is
options: Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidizing agents
Classical smog also has high concentration of oxidizing agents
solution:
A. Classical smog occurs in cool humid climate. It is a mixture of smoke, fog, and sulphur dioxide. Chemically it is a reducing mixture and so it is called reducing smog. So statement A is incorrect.

B. Classical smog is a mixture of smoke, fog and SO2. NO2 is not present in classical smog. So, statement B is incorrect.

C. Photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate. The main components of the photochemical smog result from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydrocarbons and nitrogen oxides produced by automobiles and factories. Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidising agents and is, therefore, called as oxidising smog. So, statement C is correct.

D. Classical smog is a reducing mixture, so it is called as reducing smog. Hence, statement D is incorrect.

subject: Chemistry
q: The possibility of photochemical smog formation will be minimum at
question: The possibility of photochemical smog formation will be minimum at
options: Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir in January
solution: Photochemical smog is a mixture of pollutants that are formed when nitrogen oxides and volatile organic compounds (VOCs) react to sunlight, creating a brown haze above cities. It tends to occur more often in summer, because that is when we have the most sunlight. The average temperature of Kashmir in January is estimated to be -3.6°C in Srinagar, -11.8°C in Pahalgam, and -10.4°C in Gulmarg as their minimum temperatures.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R)
Assertion (A) : Photochemical smog causes cracking of rubber.
Reason (R): Presence of ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde and peroxyacetyl nitrate in photochemical smog makes it oxidizing.
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R)
Assertion (A) : Photochemical smog causes cracking of rubber.
Reason (R): Presence of ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde and peroxyacetyl nitrate in photochemical smog makes it oxidizing.
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is not the true explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math>.

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is the true explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math>.
solution:
Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidising agents and is, therefore, called as oxidising smog.
The common components of photochemical smog are ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde and peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN). Photochemical smog causes serious health problems. Both ozone and PAN act as powerful eye irritants. Ozone and nitric oxide irritate the nose and throat and their high concentration causes headache, chest pain, dryness of the throat, cough and difficulty in breathing. Photochemical smog leads to cracking of rubber and extensive damage to plant life. It also causes corrosion of metals, stones, building materials, rubber and painted surfaces.

subject: Chemistry
q: Sulphur dioxide is one of the components of polluted air. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is also a major contributor to acid rain. The correct and complete reaction to represent acid rain caused by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is :
question: Sulphur dioxide is one of the components of polluted air. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is also a major contributor to acid rain. The correct and complete reaction to represent acid rain caused by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is :
solution:
Acid rain is a by product of a variety of human activities that emit the oxides of sulphur and nitrogen in the atmosphere. As mentioned earlier, burning of fossil fuels (which contain sulphur and nitrogenous matter) such as coal and oil in power stations and furnaces or petrol and diesel in motor engines produce sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxides. SO2 and NO2 after oxidation and reaction with water are major contributors to acid rain, because polluted air usually contains particulate matter that catalyse the oxidation.

2SO2g+O2g+2H2Ol→2H2SO4aq

4NO2g+O2g+2H2Ol→4HNO3aq

question: The industrial activity held least responsible for global warming is :
options: Electricity generation in thermal power plants.
solution: In urea industry, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>,<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo> </mo><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>  and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are consumed and no greenhouse gases are emitted. So, it is least resposible for global warming.

subject: Chemistry
q: The delicate balance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is NOT disturbed by
question: The delicate balance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is NOT disturbed by
solution:
Plants use up carbon dioxide during photosynthesis and release oxygen into the atmosphere. This maintains the delicate balance between oxygen and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.

The burning of fuels and cutting of trees is disturbing this balance in nature. As a result of such activities, the amount of carbon dioxide in the air is going up and the level of oxygen is coming down. Continuous and excessive use of fuels and deforestation would increase this imbalance. This is bringing about a warming of the atmosphere, called global warming. Respiration, is a natural process, So balance of CO2 and O2 not disturbed by respiration.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Formation of photochemical smog involves the following reaction in which A,B and C are respectively.

(i) NO2⟶huA+B

(ii) B+O2→C

(iii) A+C→NO2+O2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Formation of photochemical smog involves the following reaction in which A,B and C are respectively.

(i) NO2⟶huA+B

(ii) B+O2→C

(iii) A+C→NO2+O2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
When fossil fuels are burnt, a variety of pollutants are emitted into the earth’s troposphere. When these pollutants build up to sufficiently high levels, a chain reaction occurs from their interaction with sunlight in which NO is converted into nitrogen dioxide (NO2). This NO2 in turn absorbs energy from sunlight and breaks up into nitric oxide and free oxygen atom.

NO2 (g)→hvNO (g)+O (g)  A B-----(i)

Oxygen atoms are very reactive and combine with the O2 in air to produce ozone.

O (g)+O2 (g)⇌O3 (g)B   C-----(ii)

The ozone formed in the above reaction (ii) reacts rapidly with the NO (g) formed in reaction (i) to regenerate NO2 . NO2 is a brown gas and at sufficiently high levels can contribute to haze.

NO (g)+O3 (g)→NO2 (g)+A C   O2 (g) ----(iii)

So, the A, B and C are NO, O and O3 respectively.

can go upto
question:

subject: Chemistry
q: Which among the following pairs has only herbicides?
question: Which among the following pairs has only herbicides?
solution:
Herbicides, also commonly known as weedkillers, are chemical substances used to control unwanted plants. Selective herbicides control specific weed species, while leaving the desired crop relatively unharmed, while non-selective herbicides can be used to clear waste ground, industrial and construction sites, railways and railway embankments as they kill all plant material with which they come into contact.

Nowadays, the pesticide industry has shifted its attention to herbicides such as sodium chlorate NaClO3, sodium arsinite Na3AsO3 and many others.

subject: Chemistry
q: The deposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> on ground surfaces is referred as wet and dry depositions, respectively. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> are :
question: The deposition of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> on ground surfaces is referred as wet and dry depositions, respectively. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> are :
solution: Wet deposition is the acidic water received through rain, fog, snow, dew, mist, etc. Dry deposition is deposition of wind blown acidic gases and particles on the ground. Ammonium salt in rain drop result in wet deposition. Oxides of Nitrogen and sulphur settle down on ground as dry deposition.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: The value of the parameter "Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD)" is important for survival of aquatic life.

Statement II: The optimum value of BOD is 6.5 ppm.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
solution:
Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

For survival of aquatic life dissolved oxygen is responsible its optimum limit 6.5 ppm and optimum limit of BOD ranges from 10-20 ppm & BOD stands for biochemical oxygen demand.

subject: Chemistry
q: Air pollution that occurs in sunlight is
question: Air pollution that occurs in sunlight is
solution:
Photochemical smog, also known as 'Oxidising smog' is an atmospheric condition that produces various unwanted effects such as eye irritation and poor visibility.

The main ingredients of photochemical smog include energy from sunlight, hydrocarbons and oxides of nitrogen.

The name 'Oxidising smog' is given because it contains high concentration of oxidising agents.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion and the other is labelled as Reason.

Assertion: Polluted water may have a value of BOD of the order of 17ppm.

Reason: BOD is a measure of oxygen required to oxidise both the biodegradable and non-biodegradable organic material in water.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion and the other is labelled as Reason.

Assertion: Polluted water may have a value of BOD of the order of 17ppm.

Reason: BOD is a measure of oxygen required to oxidise both the biodegradable and non-biodegradable organic material in water.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
Assertion is false but Reason is true.
solution: The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD). The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically. Clean water would have BOD value of less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>17</mn><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> or more.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: The pH of rain water is normally ~5.6.

Statement II : If the pH of rain water drops below 5.6 , it is called acid rain.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: The pH of rain water is normally ~5.6.

Statement II : If the pH of rain water drops below 5.6 , it is called acid rain.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are true.
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is false but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is true.
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is true but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is false.
Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are false.
solution:
Both statements are correct. Normally rain water has pH of 5.6 due to the presence of H+ ions formed by the reaction of rain water with carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere.

Normal rain has a pH of about 5.6. This is because it reacts with carbon dioxide in the atmosphere and forms mildly acidic carbonic acid before it becomes rain.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following are the Green house gases?

A. Water vapour

B.Ozone

C.I2

D.Molecular hydrogen

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Which of the following are the Green house gases?

A. Water vapour

B.Ozone

C.I2

D.Molecular hydrogen

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution: The greenhouse effect is a process that occurs when gases in Earth's atmosphere trap the Sun's heat. This process makes Earth much warmer than it would be without an atmosphere. The main greenhouse gases whose concentrations are rising are carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrochlorofluorocarbons (HCFCs), hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs) and ozone in the lower atmosphere. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are not green house gases.

q: Photochemical smog consists of excessive amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>, in addition to aldehydes, ketones, peroxy acetyl nitrile <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>PAN</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo></math><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> is:
question: Photochemical smog consists of excessive amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>, in addition to aldehydes, ketones, peroxy acetyl nitrile <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi>PAN</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo></math><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> is:
solution:
Photochemical smog is formed on the chemical reaction of sunlight, nitrogen oxides and volatile organic compounds in the atmosphere.

NO2g→hvNOg+Og

Oxygen atom (nascent) is very reactive and combines with O2 in air to produce ozone.

Og+O2g→O3g

q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : Non-biodegradable wastes are generated by the thermal power plants.

Statement II : Bio-degradable detergents leads to eutrophication.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the option given below:
question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : Non-biodegradable wastes are generated by the thermal power plants.

Statement II : Bio-degradable detergents leads to eutrophication.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the option given below:
options: Both statement I and statement II are false
Statement I is true but statement II is false
Statement I is false but statement II is true
Both statement I and statement II are true.
solution: Non-biodegradable wastes are generated by the thermal power plants, which produces fly ash. Detergents which are biodegradable cause problem called eutrophication which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : In polluted water values of both dissolved oxygen and BOD are very low.

Statement II : Eutrophication results in decrease in the amount of dissolved oxygen.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : In polluted water values of both dissolved oxygen and BOD are very low.

Statement II : Eutrophication results in decrease in the amount of dissolved oxygen.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Since eutrophication is result of excessive growth of weed in water bodies, which consume dissolved oxygen of water bodies. So, Eutrophication decreases amount of dissolved oxygen in water bodies.

Polluted water has low value of dissolved oxygen, but high value of BOD (Biological oxygen demand), since more organic matter requires more dissolved oxygen to get decompose. Polluted water has BOD grater than 17.
